name: Baeckenoffa (Beef, Pork, and Lamb Stew)
recipeIngredient: 500 grams Boned shin of beef, trimmed weight (1 lb)
500 grams Boned shin of pork, trimmed weight
500 grams Boned shoulder of lamb, trimmed weight
1 1/4 kilograms Potatoes, peeled and sliced 1/2 inch
1 bouquet garni, with plenty of thyme
1 Calf's foot, blanched and cut into cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut each type of meat into 6 even pieces.
2. Place the meats in separate bowls and pour white wine over them.
3. Cover the bowls and refrigerate for at least 12 hours.
4. Preheat the oven to 160°C (325°F).
5. In a flameproof earthenware casserole, heat the goose fat over low heat.
6. Add the onions and sweat for several minutes.
7. Lay the leek on top of the onions, followed by the potatoes.
8. Add the bouquet garni, peppercorns, garlic, and arrange the meats separately on top (one type to the left, one in the middle, one to the right).
9. Pour over the remaining wine and add a light amount of salt.
10. Pour in the chicken stock.
11. Lay the pieces of calf's foot on top.
12. Place the lid on the casserole.
13. Mix the flour with a little cold water to make a soft paste.
14. Spread the paste between the top of the casserole and the lid to create an airtight seal.
15. Cook in the low oven for 4 hours.
16. Serve by breaking the seal and lifting off the lid.
